concerns.text: T027 creates data-model.md and contracts/ in Phase 6, but plan.md specifies these as Phase 1 outputs. User story tasks (T009-T026) depend on these artifacts for schema validation and entity definitions. Producer (data-model.md/contracts/) must precede consumer (US1-US3 implementation).

T033 (Generate content hashes) comes AFTER T031 (Update state YAML with artifact hashes). T031 consumes the hashes that T033 produces. This is a producer-consumer ordering violation. T033 must precede T031.

T039 implements Reference-Validator Agent in Phase 6, but Constitution Principle II requires it to run at three checkpoints including 'artifact write' and 'Advancement-Evaluator' gates. Agent must be implemented in Phase 2 (Foundational) to validate citations throughout research process, not after all validation tasks.

T015 and T016 both marked [P] but reside in same file (distribution_fitting.py). While they write to different outputs (figures/ vs processed/), shared file state could create implicit ordering requirement. Consider splitting into separate files or removing [P] tag if shared state exists.
